Elden Ring Movie Set Leak Goes Viral: First Look at A24's Game-Accurate Adaptation

Published April 5, 2026 | Gaming News

The internet has exploded with excitement after leaked footage of the Elden Ring movie set surfaced on Reddit and TikTok , giving fans their first real glimpse of A24's highly anticipated live-action adaptation. TikTok user Throxtv captured videos revealing what appears to be the set of Limgrave, including the infamous Church of Marika – complete with a statue inside .

For Tarnished warriors who have been waiting patiently since the film was announced in May 2025 , this leak is nothing short of a resurrection at a Site of Grace. The footage shows construction work on what fans immediately recognized as one of the game's most iconic locations, and it looks absolutely stunning.

Table of Contents

  1. The Viral Leak: What We Saw
  2. Breaking Down the Set Details
  3. Alex Garland's Vision for the Lands Between
  4. Confirmed Cast and Crew
  5. Why Elden Ring Became a Cultural Phenomenon
  6. Production Timeline and Release Expectations
  7. A24's Growing Video Game Empire
  8. What This Means for Filipino Gamers

The Viral Leak: What We Saw

The footage appears to have been recorded by a fan somewhere in the UK, and was originally posted to TikTok by user throxtv . Within hours, the videos had spread across social media platforms like wildfire through the Caelid Swamp.

The Numbers Behind the Viral Phenomenon

The leak's reach has been absolutely massive:

  • The leaked footage generated close to 175,000 views shortly after posting
  • A TikTok clip allegedly showing a Church of Marika set has racked up 25,000 upvotes on Reddit
  • Since being shared online, the videos have gone viral, racking up over two million views apiece
Note: While A24 hasn't officially confirmed the footage's authenticity, multiple sources have verified the set's existence and location markers suggest legitimate production work.

What Makes This Leak So Exciting

Unlike generic set photos, this leak shows something truly special. The footage showcases an in-the-works set with the Statue of Marika and a stone structure that fans say looks like "was literally ripped straight from the game" . The level of detail is what's getting fans hyped—this isn't just inspired by Elden Ring, it's a faithful recreation.

Kotaku has been able to speak to the original uploader of the clip and track down another video of the set , lending further credibility to the leak. The leak appears to have started with TikTok user THROX, also known as @throxtv, who posted video from a fenced-off site in the English countryside. Throx followed up the leak with another video from the same location, lending more weight to the footage .

Breaking Down the Set Details

The Church of Marika Recreation

In the clips, fans quickly spotted what appears to be a near-exact recreation of the Church of Marika, including a large statue resembling Queen Marika at the center of the ruins . For those who've played Elden Ring, these churches serve as crucial lore locations scattered throughout The Lands Between.

The open field it's sitting in resembles the game's opening area, Limgrave, minus the fences surrounding it . This suggests the film may follow a similar narrative path to the game, starting players—err, viewers—in the same beginner area.

Production Quality and Construction

Sharp-eyed fans noticed professional construction equipment and branding on-site. Filming appears to be taking place in the UK, as evidenced both by the scenery and the easily identifiable Hedgehog Construction logo on some of the construction work. The Surrey-based company is often hired to help with set assembly on television and movie projects .

In a follow-up video, Throxtv showed off the opposite side of the church and the other props nearby. Wagons, barrels, and stacks of cinderblocks can be seen, along with construction equipment .

What the Set Reveals About the Story

Hardcore lore enthusiasts have been analyzing every frame. The Church of Marika shown in the clip appears in a post-Shattering state, meaning broken and abandoned rather than intact. That points toward the movie being set after the Shattering event rather than serving as a prequel .

This is significant because many fans hoped for a prequel exploring George R.R. Martin's original worldbuilding. Whether the film follows a straight adaptation of the Tarnished's journey or tells an adjacent story within the same world remains completely open .

Spoiler Alert: If you haven't played Elden Ring yet, be aware that story discussions may reveal plot points from the game.

Alex Garland's Vision for the Lands Between

Why Alex Garland Is Perfect for Elden Ring

Garland, known for his cerebral and visually striking work — which includes "Ex Machina," "Annihilation," and "Civil War" — is a confirmed FromSoftware superfan. His track record with atmospheric, philosophical sci-fi makes him an ideal choice for Elden Ring's dark fantasy world.

The director's dedication is legendary. The Elden Ring movie was approved by Hidetaka Miyazaki himself, after director Alex Garland flew to Japan and presented him with a 160-page draft of his vision . That's not a treatment—that's a full screenplay pitched directly to the creator.

Reports in 2025 said Garland pitched the project with a 160-page script and 40 pages of imagery before flying to Japan with A24 to secure approval from FromSoftware and Bandai Namco . This level of preparation convinced fans that the film was landing with someone who genuinely loves the source material.

Garland's Recent Work

On top of that, he recently revived the "28 Days Later" franchise with "28 Years Later" and its sequel, "The Bone Temple," both of which he wrote and is producing . Despite hints at retirement, Garland is clearly not slowing down.

Check out more about gaming merchandise and collectibles to celebrate your favorite FromSoftware titles while waiting for the film.

Confirmed Cast and Crew

The Leading Trio

Casting includes Kit Connor ("Heartstopper"), Ben Whishaw ("Skyfall"), and Cailee Spaeny ("Priscilla," "Alien: Romulus"), who are all said to have the three lead roles .

This casting is particularly exciting because:

Actor Known For Why They Fit Elden Ring
Cailee Spaeny Civil War, Alien: Romulus This is the third time Spaeny and Garland have worked together. First on the FX series Devs, then on last year's hit film Civil War which made $127M globally
Kit Connor Heartstopper Rising star with proven emotional range
Ben Whishaw Skyfall, Paddington Veteran actor capable of nuanced performances

Production Team

The Elden Ring film is being produced in collaboration between A24 and Bandai Namco. The film has already attracted some serious talent, with writer and director Alex Garland signing on to lead the project .

Even original Elden Ring contributor George R. R. Martin has praised his work, describing him as a "first rate director" . Coming from the man who co-created the game's lore, that's high praise indeed.

Why Elden Ring Became a Cultural Phenomenon

Sales Numbers That Shattered Expectations

To understand why this movie matters, you need to grasp just how massive Elden Ring became. As of April 2025, global unit sales of action RPG Elden Ring stood at 30 million units . That's not just good—that's legendary.

Released in 2022 and co-created with George R. R. Martin, "Elden Ring" sold more than 30 million copies worldwide and was crowned Game of the Year . The punishing action RPG hit the ground running with a meteoric 12 million sales in just 2 weeks .

Critical Acclaim and Awards

The critically acclaimed action RPG has more than 30 million unit sales and 300 "Game of the Year" nominations and wins, including winning at The Game Awards, DICE, and Golden Joystick Award .

The franchise continues to expand: ELDEN RING Shadow of the Erdtree, the DLC expansion to the original game recently surpassed more than 10 million units worldwide .

Player Engagement Is Off the Charts

Sales are one thing, but player dedication is another. Seven million Steam players – 44.7% of Elden Ring's Steam audience – have played for over 100 hours . Nearly half the player base has invested that much time! Almost 700K players across PlayStation (2.7%) and Steam (2.1%) have played Elden Ring for over 500 hours .

Production Timeline and Release Expectations

When Filming Begins

Casting includes Kit Connor ("Heartstopper"), Ben Whishaw ("Skyfall"), and Cailee Spaeny ("Priscilla," "Alien: Romulus"), who are all said to have the three lead roles , and with sets now being constructed, production appears imminent.

If sets are being erected that means filming is imminent, so we should learn a lot more soon . A tarp has since been put around the installation to prevent prying eyes from seeing it , suggesting A24 wants to keep things under wraps now that the leak has gone viral.

Expected Release Window

It's likely the film won't see the light of day until 2027 at earliest, and the recently leaked movie set footage is a good indication that the ball is already rolling in the right direction in terms of production timeline .

The Elden Ring film does not have a confirmed release date at this point , but industry insiders suggest late 2027 is realistic given the production complexity.

Timeline Summary:
  • May 2025: Film officially announced
  • July 2025: Main cast negotiations reported
  • April 2026: Set construction leaked
  • Estimated: Late 2026/Early 2027 filming
  • Projected Release: Late 2027 or 2028

A24's Growing Video Game Empire

Why A24 Is Perfect for Game Adaptations

A24 has built its reputation on auteur-driven, visually stunning films that take risks. A24's move into adapting video games is part of a growing trend for the studio to embrace video game content. Although Netflix has more experience with game-based adaptations—having produced hits like Castlevania, Arcane, and recently Devil May Cry—A24 is steadily carving out its own niche .

Other A24 Game Projects

The indie studio first made waves by announcing a film adaptation of Hideo Kojima's Death Stranding, with Michael Sarnoski attached to direct . That's another beloved game getting the prestige treatment.

Earlier in 2025, A24 surprised fans by revealing plans for a movie based on the critically acclaimed game Elden Ring. This project is to be helmed by Alex Garland, a frequent A24 collaborator and devoted fan of FromSoftware's work .

The Growing Southeast Asian Gaming Market

The Philippines has one of the fastest-growing gaming communities in Southeast Asia. Elden Ring's success here mirrors its global performance, with Filipino Tarnished conquering Malenia and debating lore on social media just like everyone else.

This film adaptation represents something bigger: mainstream Hollywood is finally treating video game stories with the respect they deserve. No more cheap cash-grabs—we're getting A-list talent, serious budgets, and creative teams who actually care about the source material.

Where to Watch When It Releases

While we're still years away from release, Filipino fans can expect theatrical distribution through major cinema chains. A24 films typically get wide releases, especially for high-profile projects like this.

Community Reactions

Fans of the FromSoftware RPG are particularly impressed with how authentic everything looks . Filipino gaming communities on Facebook and Discord have been buzzing with excitement, creating fan castings and debating which bosses will make it into the film.

The consensus? Cautious optimism. Garland's involvement and the leaked set quality suggest this could be the video game adaptation that finally gets it right.

Frequently Asked Questions

When will the Elden Ring movie be released?

While no official release date has been announced, industry experts predict a late 2027 or 2028 theatrical release. The leaked set footage from April 2026 suggests filming will begin soon, with typical post-production timelines placing the release about 18-24 months later.

Who is directing the Elden Ring movie?

Alex Garland (Ex Machina, Annihilation, Civil War) is writing and directing the Elden Ring film. He personally pitched the project to Hidetaka Miyazaki with a 160-page screenplay and 40 pages of imagery, demonstrating his passion for the source material.

What is the Elden Ring movie's plot?

The exact plot remains unknown, but leaked set footage showing a post-Shattering Church of Marika suggests the film takes place after the Shattering event, similar to the game's timeline. It may follow the Tarnished's journey or tell an original story within The Lands Between.

Who are the actors in the Elden Ring movie?

The main cast reportedly includes Kit Connor (Heartstopper), Ben Whishaw (Skyfall), and Cailee Spaeny (Alien: Romulus, Civil War) in the three lead roles. Specific character assignments haven't been officially confirmed.

Is the leaked Elden Ring set footage real?

While A24 hasn't officially confirmed it, multiple sources have verified the footage's authenticity. The set features branding from Hedgehog Construction, a UK company known for film and television set construction, and TikTok user Throxtv has posted multiple videos from the location.

The Tarnished Path Forward

The viral Elden Ring movie set leak marks an exciting milestone for one of gaming's most anticipated adaptations. With Alex Garland's proven directorial vision, A24's commitment to quality storytelling, and a talented cast bringing The Lands Between to life, this film has all the ingredients for success.

If these first set images are any indication of what's to come, good things are on the horizon . The attention to detail, the game-accurate recreations, and the serious talent involved suggest this won't be another forgettable video game movie—it could be something truly special.

For Filipino gamers who've spent hundreds of hours exploring every corner of The Lands Between, defeating impossible bosses, and diving deep into the lore, seeing this world realized on the big screen will be a dream come true. Whether you're Team Ranni, a Golden Order loyalist, or just here to watch things burn with the Frenzied Flame, we'll all be lining up for tickets when this film finally arrives.

The set leak proves what we already suspected: Alex Garland and A24 aren't just making an Elden Ring movie—they're crafting a love letter to FromSoftware's masterpiece. And if these early glimpses are any indication, it's going to be maidenless no more.

Elden Ring Movie Set Leak: First Look at Alex Garland's A24 Adaptation Goes Viral

Published on April 5, 2026 | By Smartly.sale Gaming Team

The internet is ablaze with excitement after leaked footage of the Elden Ring movie set surfaced on TikTok and Reddit , offering fans the first real glimpse of what promises to be one of gaming's most ambitious film adaptations. The viral videos showcase a stunning recreation of the Church of Marika that looks "was literally ripped straight from the game" according to fans who've analyzed the footage frame by frame.

For Filipino gamers and Elden Ring fans worldwide who've spent hundreds of hours exploring The Lands Between, this leak represents a watershed moment—proof that Alex Garland and A24 are taking the source material seriously. With 30 million units sold worldwide as of April 2025 , Elden Ring has become a cultural phenomenon, and this movie adaptation could bring FromSoftware's dark fantasy world to an even broader audience.

Table of Contents

  1. The Leaked Footage: What We're Seeing
  2. Breaking Down the Set Details

  8. Frequently Asked Questions

The Leaked Footage: What We're Seeing

The footage was originally posted to TikTok by user throxtv , who appears to have stumbled upon the set while traveling through the British countryside. Within hours, the clips spread like wildfire across social media platforms, racking up 25,000 upvotes on Reddit and accumulating close to 175,000 views across platforms.

Authenticity of the Leak

While there's no official word that the movie has started filming, the footage is extremely compelling and is unquestionably Elden Ring-related . Several factors point to the legitimacy of these leaks:

  • Construction Company Evidence: Cinderblocks visible in follow-up footage bear the logo of Hedgehog Construction, a company known for providing "specialist scenery construction for film and television"
  • Multiple Angles: TikTok user THROX followed up the leak with another video from the same location , showing different perspectives of the set
  • Production Continuity: The presence of wagons, barrels, and construction equipment typical of active film sets
Note: As of this writing, A24 has not released any kind of statement confirming or denying that this is a production set for the movie . However, the evidence strongly suggests these are genuine production sets for the upcoming adaptation.

What the Footage Shows

The video allegedly shows a Church of Marika set, built somewhere in the British countryside, presenting what looks like a near-exact physical recreation of one of Elden Ring's most recognizable points of interest . The level of detail is remarkable, with the environment closely resembling the Gothic architecture and visual style seen in the original game .

Breaking Down the Set Details

The Church of Marika Statue

The centerpiece of the leaked footage is undoubtedly the Marika statue. Many commenters on Reddit and social media are already praising the statue for looking like it was adapted straight from the video game . This attention to detail suggests that Alex Garland and his team are committed to maintaining the visual authenticity that made Elden Ring such a memorable experience.

Location and Setting Analysis

Fans have been meticulously comparing the leaked footage to in-game locations. The set is absolutely a church of Marika, with a statue that is more or less intact compared to broken ones in the DLC, and specifically looks like it could be the third church of Marika . Some interesting observations include:

  • The open field resembles the game's opening area, Limgrave, minus the fences surrounding it
  • The Church appears in a post-Shattering state, broken and abandoned, pointing toward the movie being set after the Shattering event
  • The crumbling stonework matches the aesthetic of The Lands Between perfectly

Production Quality Indicators

Element Details Significance
Statue Scale Full-size physical recreation Shows commitment to practical effects over CGI
Stone Architecture Weathered, detailed stonework Game-accurate visual fidelity
Location Choice UK countryside Natural landscapes matching Limgrave's rolling hills
Set Size Large-scale outdoor construction Indicates substantial production budget
Gamer Tip: If you're replaying Elden Ring to compare locations, the Churches of Marika serve as grace sites throughout The Lands Between. There are multiple churches scattered across different regions, each in various states of ruin.

Elden Ring Movie: Production Status & Timeline

Official Announcement and Development

Bandai Namco officially announced the Elden Ring movie in May 2025, confirming that Garland would write and direct the adaptation as part of a collaboration with A24 . The announcement sent shockwaves through both gaming and film communities, as it brought together one of the most successful games of the decade with one of Hollywood's most prestigious independent studios.

Alex Garland's Passion Project

What sets this adaptation apart is the director's genuine enthusiasm for the source material. Reports in 2025 said Garland pitched the project with a 160-page script and 40 pages of imagery before flying to Japan with A24 to secure approval from FromSoftware and Bandai Namco . This level of preparation demonstrates that Garland isn't just taking on another IP adaptation—he's deeply invested in honoring the game's legacy.

The filmmaker whipped up a 160-page script just to convince FromSoftware and Bandai Namco that he should be the one to handle it , showcasing his dedication to the project.

Expected Release Timeline

While no official release date has been announced, industry analysts are making educated guesses based on production timelines. The film likely won't see the light of day until 2027 at earliest , though according to current estimates, the picture is unlikely to be released before 2027 .

Warning: Be cautious of fake "release date" announcements circulating on social media. No official premiere date has been confirmed by A24 or Bandai Namco as of April 2026.

Production Company Collaboration

Bandai Namco Entertainment Inc. and A24 announced their collaboration with writer and director Alex Garland for a live-action film adaptation . This partnership brings together:

  • A24: Known for acclaimed films like Ex Machina, Hereditary, and Everything Everywhere All at Once
  • Bandai Namco: Publisher with deep understanding of the Elden Ring IP
  • FromSoftware: The creative minds behind the original game, providing consultation

Cast, Crew, and Creative Team

Director: Alex Garland

The project will be led by filmmaker Alex Garland (Ex Machina, Civil War, Warfare) as writer and director . Garland's filmography demonstrates his ability to handle complex, atmospheric storytelling—skills that will be essential for bringing The Lands Between to life.

His previous works include:

  • Ex Machina (2014) - Thoughtful sci-fi exploring AI consciousness
  • Annihilation (2018) - Atmospheric horror with cosmic themes
  • Civil War (2024) - Intense political thriller
  • Warfare (2025) - Recent A24 collaboration

Producers and Creative Consultants

The film will be produced by Peter Rice alongside Andrew Macdonald and Allon Reich from DNA, and George R. R. Martin and Vince Gerardis . Having George R.R. Martin involved as a producer is particularly significant, as Martin had a considerable hand in shaping The Lands Between, penning much of the world's history and backstory .

Rumored Cast

While no actors have been officially confirmed, industry insiders have reported casting developments. Kit Connor is being eyed for the lead role , according to Deadline. Additionally, actors Ben Whishaw (Skyfall), Kit Connor (Heartstopper), and Cailee Spaeny (Civil War) were "in talks" to star in the film .

Actor Known For Rumored Role
Kit Connor Heartstopper, Warfare Lead role (possibly a Tarnished)
Ben Whishaw Skyfall, Paddington Undisclosed
Cailee Spaeny Civil War, Priscilla Undisclosed
Casting Note: Right now, no one has been officially cast, nor do we know which part of The Lands Between's history Alex Garland has chosen to adapt . All casting reports remain unconfirmed speculation at this stage.

Key Creative Considerations

Queen Marika is expected to be a focal point of the Elden Ring movie, as she is essentially the god and ruler of The Lands Between, the driving force behind much of the land's conflicts . This makes narrative sense given the prominence of Marika statues in the leaked footage.

Why Elden Ring Matters: The Game's Massive Success

Sales Milestones and Industry Impact

To understand why this movie adaptation matters, we need to look at the unprecedented success of the source material. As of April 2025, global unit sales of action RPG Elden Ring stood at 30 million units . This makes it one of the most popular new games in recent memory, and one of the best-performing new IPs in gaming history .

Breaking Down the Numbers

  • Launch Performance: The game hit the ground running with a meteoric 12 million sales in just 2 weeks
  • First Year: After a year on the market, Elden Ring had moved 20 million units
  • DLC Success: Shadow of the Erdtree, the DLC expansion, recently surpassed more than 10 million units worldwide
  • Franchise Expansion: The Elden Ring franchise has now surpassed 46 million total cumulative sales across the main game, Shadow of the Erdtree, and Nightreign

Critical Acclaim and Awards

After its release, Elden Ring became a sweeping success at the 2022 Game Awards, taking home prizes for game of the year, best game direction and best role playing game . The game has received more than 300 "Game of the Year" nominations and wins, including winning at The Game Awards, DICE, and Golden Joystick Award .

Cultural Phenomenon in the Philippines

Filipino gamers have embraced Elden Ring with particular enthusiasm. The game's challenging but rewarding gameplay loop resonates with the competitive gaming culture in the Philippines. Many local streamers and content creators built substantial followings by creating Elden Ring guides, lore explanations, and challenge runs—content that remains popular even years after release.

For Filipino Gamers: Elden Ring's success has opened doors for more complex, narrative-driven games in the Southeast Asian market. The film adaptation could further boost interest in FromSoftware titles, potentially leading to more localized content and regional events.

What to Expect from the Adaptation

Story Direction and Timeline

One of the biggest questions surrounding the adaptation is which part of Elden Ring's story it will tell. There's been no official talk so far about Elden Ring adapting the story of FromSoftware's 2023 classic, nor the side narrative found in the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC .

Based on the leaked set footage, some fans speculate about the timeline. The destroyed ruins suggest the movie is set after the Shattering event rather than serving as a prequel depicting The Lands Between before Ranni stole a fragment of the Rune of Death . However, whether the film follows a straight adaptation of the Tarnished's journey or tells an adjacent story within the same world remains completely open .

The Challenge of Adapting Elden Ring

Gamers who have played the epic and punishing FromSoftware hit know that dialogue is minimal in the game, but that there is plenty of worldbuilding to go around. Adapting the story into a live-action movie definitely seems like a challenge .

Key adaptation challenges include:

  • Environmental Storytelling: Much of Elden Ring's narrative is conveyed through item descriptions, environmental details, and visual cues
  • Player Agency: The game's story changes based on player choices and which NPCs they interact with
  • Scale: The Lands Between is massive, with multiple regions, each with distinct aesthetics and lore
  • Tone Balance: Maintaining the dark, melancholic atmosphere while making the story accessible to general audiences

George R.R. Martin's Involvement

ELDEN RING was created under the guidance of FromSoftware's Hidetaka Miyazaki, based on a mythological story written by George R. R. Martin . Having Martin as a producer ensures continuity with the game's foundational lore, which could help navigate the adaptation's storytelling challenges.

Production Scale and Budget

While specific budget figures haven't been disclosed, the project is expected to be one of A24's largest productions to date . The scale of the leaked set pieces—full-size statues and elaborate stonework—suggests a substantial investment in practical effects and production design.

Visual Style and Cinematography

Alex Garland's previous films demonstrate a mastery of atmospheric cinematography. Expect:

  • Practical effects blended with CGI for creature designs
  • Moody, desaturated color palettes matching the game's aesthetic
  • Epic landscape shots showcasing The Lands Between's vistas
  • Careful attention to armor and weapon design accuracy

Fan Reactions and Speculation

Social Media Explodes

The leaked footage has fans absolutely hyped for what's to come from Alex Garland and A24 when further official details about the Elden Ring movie start to trickle out . The Reddit Elden Ring community in particular has been dissecting every frame of the footage, comparing architectural details to in-game screenshots.

Community Theories

Fans have developed several theories based on the leaked footage:

  • Timeline Theory: The post-Shattering setting suggests we might see the aftermath of the war between demigods
  • Protagonist Theory: The focus on Marika suggests she could be a central character, possibly in flashback sequences
  • Location Theory: The area itself looks a lot like Limgrave , suggesting the film might start in the same region as the game
  • Scope Theory: The scale of practical sets indicates major action sequences and battle scenes

Optimism vs. Skepticism

The gaming community's reaction has been cautiously optimistic. If these first set images are any indication of what's to come, good things are on the horizon . However, fans remain aware of the challenges inherent in video game adaptations.

Positive indicators:

  • Alex Garland's proven track record with thoughtful, atmospheric storytelling
  • A24's commitment to artistic integrity over commercial formulas
  • George R.R. Martin's direct involvement as producer
  • Evidence of game-accurate production design

Global Impact and Philippine Gaming Community

The leak has generated particular excitement in the Philippines, where Elden Ring maintains a dedicated player base. Local gaming communities on Facebook and Discord have been sharing the footage extensively, with Filipino content creators already planning coverage strategies for when official trailers and announcements arrive.

Community Engagement: Filipino Elden Ring fans have created comparison videos on YouTube, translation guides for lore, and community events centered around the game. This movie could revitalize interest and bring new players into the FromSoftware ecosystem.

If filming is genuinely underway in the UK as the clip implies, more sightings should surface soon. A24 productions shot openly enough in the past that set leaks have preceded major announcements before. Keep an eye on the Elden Ring subreddit and official channels from A24 and Bandai Namco for additional updates.

When will the Elden Ring movie be released?

While no official release date has been announced, industry analysts suggest the film won't premiere before 2027 at the earliest. A24 and Bandai Namco have not confirmed any timeline as of April 2026.

Alex Garland is both writing and directing the Elden Ring adaptation. Garland is known for acclaimed films like Ex Machina, Annihilation, and Civil War. metaTitle: Elden Ring Movie Set Leak Goes Viral: Church of Marika Statue Spotted
metaDescription: Viral leaked footage reveals stunning Church of Marika set from Alex Garland's A24 Elden Ring movie. Get the latest details, release date speculation & more.
excerpt: Leaked set footage from the highly anticipated Elden Ring movie has gone viral, showing a game-accurate Church of Marika statue and ruins. The footage confirms production is underway on Alex Garland's A24 adaptation, set for 2027 release.
content:
Breaking: Leaked set footage from the Elden Ring movie has surfaced online, showing what appears to be a game-accurate Church of Marika statue. The viral videos have generated over 175,000 views and confirm production is underway in the UK.

The gaming world is buzzing after newly surfaced fan-recorded footage of the Elden Ring movie set has started to circulate on various social media channels such as Reddit and TikTok . The leaked videos show what fans immediately recognized as an incredibly detailed Church of Marika set piece, complete with the iconic statue of Queen Marika that's central to FromSoftware's critically acclaimed action RPG.

For Filipino gamers and Elden Ring fans who've spent countless hours exploring The Lands Between, this leak represents the first concrete evidence that the highly anticipated A24 film adaptation is moving from concept to reality. Bandai Namco officially announced the Elden Ring movie in May 2025, confirming that Garland would write and direct the adaptation as part of a collaboration with A24 , but until now, we've had no visual proof of production.

The timing couldn't be more perfect for Elden Ring fans in the Philippines and worldwide. With the game having sold 30 million units worldwide , and the franchise continuing to expand with the recent Nightreign spinoff, the live-action adaptation represents FromSoftware's boldest transmedia move yet.

Table of Contents

  1. What the Leaked Set Footage Reveals
  2. How Fans Are Reacting to the Leak
  3. Elden Ring Movie Production Status
  4. Alex Garland's Vision for The Lands Between
  5. Cast, Plot, and Timeline Speculation
  6. Why Practical Sets Matter for Elden Ring
  7. When Will the Elden Ring Movie Release?
  8. What This Means for Filipino Gamers

What the Leaked Set Footage Reveals

The leak appears to have started with TikTok user THROX, also known as @throxtv, who posted video from a fenced-off site in the English countryside . The footage has since exploded across gaming communities, with the TikTok clip allegedly showing a Church of Marika set racking up 25,000 upvotes on Reddit .

Game-Accurate Church of Marika Design

What's immediately striking about the leaked footage is how faithfully it recreates the game's aesthetic. The footage showcases an in-the-works set with the Statue of Marika and a stone structure that fans such as omgItsGhostDog say looks like "was literally ripped straight from the game" . This level of accuracy has fans cautiously optimistic that A24 and director Alex Garland understand the assignment.

The footage presents what looks like a near-exact physical recreation of one of Elden Ring's most recognizable points of interest—the Church of Marika, scattered across The Lands Between in various states of ruin . For players who've discovered these haunting locations during their journey, seeing them recreated in physical form is surreal.

Additional Set Details Spotted

Beyond the central Marika statue, eagle-eyed fans have identified other elements from the game. Other details visible in the footage include crucifixion stakes similar to the grim scenery seen across Limgrave, as well as extra props such as barrels and wooden carriages . These atmospheric details suggest the production team is committed to bringing the game's dark fantasy world to life with meticulous attention to detail.

In a follow-up video, Throxtv showed off the opposite side of the church and the other props nearby, including wagons, barrels, and stacks of cinderblocks, along with construction equipment . Professional construction materials confirmed the legitimacy of the set— the cinderblocks bear the logo of Hedgehog Construction, a company known for providing "specialist scenery construction for film and television" .

Pro Tip: If you're an Elden Ring fan wanting to revisit the Churches of Marika before the movie drops, check out our gaming deals section for discounts on Elden Ring and the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC.

How Fans Are Reacting to the Leak

The gaming community's response to the leaked footage has been overwhelmingly positive, with many expressing surprise at the production's commitment to practical effects and game accuracy.

Social Media Explosion

The leaked footage appeared on Reddit and TikTok with the caption "Elden Ring movie set spotted" and has already generated close to 175,000 views . This represents just a fraction of the overall engagement when you factor in shares, reposts, and discussion threads across platforms like Twitter/X, Instagram, and gaming forums.

The leaked footage has fans absolutely hyped for what's to come from Alex Garland and A24 when further official details about the Elden Ring movie start to trickle out . Comments sections are filled with cautious optimism, with many pointing to A24's track record of quality filmmaking as reason for hope.

Debate Over Adaptation Approach

Not everyone is convinced, however. The footage officially confirms that the movie will be live-action rather than animation, and there had been many Elden Ring players who felt that animation would more effectively capture the vibe of Elden Ring . Some fans argue that the game's fantastical elements—including massive demigods, cosmic horror, and impossible architecture—might be difficult to realize convincingly in live-action.

There's also ongoing debate about format. Other fans have also lamented how A24 is adapting Elden Ring as a feature film as opposed to a TV series, given the abundance of lore found in the original game . With George R.R. Martin's extensive worldbuilding and the game's complex mythology, some believe a multi-season series would better serve the material.

Elden Ring Movie Production Status

While A24 and Bandai Namco haven't officially commented on the leaked footage, the evidence suggests production is well underway in the United Kingdom.

Filming Location Confirmed

The footage appears to have been recorded by a fan somewhere in the UK, and was originally posted to TikTok by user throxtv earlier today . The UK location makes logistical sense—it offers experienced film crews, tax incentives for productions, and landscapes that naturally evoke the game's medieval fantasy aesthetic.

Production is now underway, with reports indicating that filming is taking place in the United Kingdom . The choice to shoot in Britain rather than relying entirely on soundstages suggests Garland wants to ground the fantastical elements in real locations.

No Official Statement Yet

At this time, the videos have not been taken down and A24 has not released any kind of statement confirming or denying that this is a production set for the movie . This silence is typical for early-stage productions, though the lack of takedown notices might indicate the studio is comfortable with the free marketing these leaks provide.

Note: While the leaked footage appears legitimate, always be cautious about unverified information. Wait for official announcements from A24, Bandai Namco, or director Alex Garland before treating any production details as confirmed.

Alex Garland's Vision for The Lands Between

The choice of Alex Garland as writer-director has been one of the most encouraging aspects of this adaptation from the start.

Garland's Gaming Credentials

Reports in 2025 said Garland pitched the project with a 160-page script and 40 pages of imagery before flying to Japan with A24 to secure approval from FromSoftware and Bandai Namco . This level of preparation demonstrates genuine passion for the source material—Garland didn't just pitch an idea, he arrived with a complete vision.

The filmmaker is a noted fan of the game, so much so that he whipped up a 160-page script just to convince FromSoftware and Bandai Namco that he should be the one to handle it . For Filipino fans worried about another soulless video game adaptation, Garland's fandom credentials are reassuring.

Perfect Director for Dark Fantasy

Known for films such as Ex Machina and Annihilation, Garland is expected to bring a distinct tone to the adaptation . His previous work demonstrates mastery of cerebral science fiction with horror elements—exactly the sensibilities needed for Elden Ring's obtuse storytelling and cosmic dread.

Garland's films typically favor practical effects over CGI whenever possible, which aligns perfectly with what we're seeing in these set leaks. His preference for atmosphere and implied horror rather than explicit spectacle could translate beautifully to Elden Ring's environmental storytelling approach.

George R.R. Martin's Involvement

George R.R. Martin, who contributed to the game's lore, is also involved as a creative consultant . This ensures continuity between the game's mythology and the film's narrative, though it also means Filipino fans hoping for a quick release might need to manage expectations—Martin's projects aren't exactly known for speed.

Cast, Plot, and Timeline Speculation

One of the biggest mysteries surrounding the Elden Ring film is what story it will actually tell.

Potential Cast Members

Independent entertainment journalist Jeff Sneider reported last year that actors Ben Whishaw (Skyfall), Kit Connor (Heartstopper), and Cailee Spaeny (Civil War) were "in talks" to star in the film . None of these casting rumors have been officially confirmed, but all three actors would bring serious dramatic chops to The Lands Between.

Since the announcement, we've not actually heard too much about the project, beyond a few casting announcements (including Ben Whishaw and Cailee Spaeny, who have been cast in undisclosed roles) . The "undisclosed roles" part is crucial—we don't yet know if they'll play recognizable characters from the game or entirely new creations.

Post-Shattering Setting Likely

The leaked set footage itself provides clues about the film's timeline. The Church of Marika shown in the clip appears in a post-Shattering state, meaning broken and abandoned rather than intact, which points toward the movie being set after the Shattering event rather than serving as a prequel .

This suggests we might see a story similar to the game's protagonist—a Tarnished returning to The Lands Between after the Elden Ring's destruction. However, whether the film follows a straight adaptation of the Tarnished's journey or tells an adjacent story within the same world remains completely open .

What Story Makes Sense?

For Filipino gamers wondering how you adapt a game known for minimal dialogue and player-driven narrative, the answer likely lies in the lore. George R.R. Martin had a considerable hand in shaping The Lands Between, penning much of the world's history and backstory—essentially, everything before the player's Tarnished arrives is the product of the Game of Thrones author .

This gives Garland rich material to work with. The film could explore the war against the giants, Godfrey's conquest, Marika's rise to godhood, or the events immediately following the Shattering. Any of these would provide dramatic narrative structure while maintaining the game's mysterious atmosphere.

Why Practical Sets Matter for Elden Ring

The decision to build physical sets rather than relying entirely on green screens and CGI is significant for the film's potential success.

Authenticity and Immersion

If the set is genuine, it suggests Garland's adaptation is leaning heavily into the look of the Lands Between and using practical builds to bring some of Elden Ring's most recognizable imagery to life . This approach grounds the fantastical elements in tangible reality, which should help actors deliver more grounded performances.

Images and clips circulating on social media appear to show large-scale set construction, including a detailed replica of Queen Marika's statue, with the environment closely resembling the Gothic architecture and visual style seen in the original game . This attention to detail suggests A24 understands what makes Elden Ring's visual design so memorable.

Learning from Other Game Adaptations

The best recent video game adaptations—The Last of Us, Fallout, Arcane—succeeded because they respected the source material's aesthetic while adapting the narrative for a new medium. By building practical Churches of Marika, the Elden Ring film demonstrates similar respect for FromSoftware's artistic vision.

For comparison, check out our coverage of other gaming adaptations and how they've handled the transition from interactive to passive media on our gaming news hub.

When Will the Elden Ring Movie Release?

While no official release date has been announced, we can make educated guesses based on production timelines and industry standards.

Earliest Possible Release

Neither the plot, nor the cast, nor the exact release dates have been revealed, though according to current estimates, the picture is unlikely to be released before 2027 . This makes sense given the film was only announced in May 2025 and principal photography appears to be in early stages.

It's likely the film won't see the light of day until 2027 at earliest, and the recently leaked movie set footage is a good indication that the ball is already rolling in the right direction in terms of production timeline . For a film with this level of complexity—practical effects, likely extensive post-production, and Garland's meticulous approach—a 2027 theatrical release would be ambitious.

Production Timeline Considerations

Given that the project was only announced in May 2025, it sounds like production may be underway according to some leaked set footage that appeared on Reddit and TikTok . If we assume 6-12 months of principal photography, followed by 12-18 months of post-production, a late 2027 or early 2028 release becomes more realistic.

Production Phase Estimated Timeline Status
Script Development 2024-2025 Complete
Pre-Production Late 2025 Complete
Principal Photography Early-Mid 2026 In Progress
Post-Production/VFX Late 2026-2027 Upcoming
Theatrical Release 2027-2028 TBD

Festival Circuit Possibilities

Given A24's track record, don't be surprised if the Elden Ring film premieres at a major festival like Cannes, Venice, or Sundance before wide theatrical release. This would generate critical buzz and award season momentum—crucial for a video game adaptation trying to establish legitimacy.

What This Means for Filipino Gamers

For the passionate Elden Ring community in the Philippines, this leak represents both exciting confirmation and a call for cautious optimism.

A Global Gaming Phenomenon Comes to Film

Elden Ring's success in the Philippines mirrors its worldwide domination. Developed by FromSoftware and released by Bandai Namco Entertainment, this game has shipped more than 30 million units worldwide since its launch . Filipino gamers have contributed significantly to that number, making Elden Ring one of the most-played titles in local gaming cafes and among home console owners.

The game was massively successful, going on to sell over 30 million copies to become one of the best-selling titles ever, and won Game of the Year at The Game Awards 2022 . This commercial and critical success makes the film adaptation both more likely to succeed and more risky—expectations are stratospheric.

Potential Philippine Theatrical Release

A24 films typically receive wide international distribution, meaning Filipino fans should expect the Elden Ring movie to screen in major cinemas across Metro Manila, Cebu, Davao, and other cities. The question is whether it will receive simultaneous global release or follow a staggered rollout.

Expanding the Elden Ring Universe

The film is just one part of Elden Ring's ongoing expansion. ELDEN RING NIGHTREIGN released back in May of this year as a standalone multiplayer co-op action survival game, selling more than 2 million units less than 24 hours after becoming publicly available . The franchise's multi-platform approach—games, DLC, spinoffs, and now film—mirrors successful transmedia properties like The Witcher.

Final Thoughts: Cautious Optimism for The Lands Between

The leaked Elden Ring movie set footage provides the first tangible evidence that Alex Garland's vision for The Lands Between is becoming reality. The game-accurate Church of Marika, complete with its haunting statue and crumbling ruins, demonstrates a level of care and attention to detail that should reassure nervous fans.

However, fans have no clue what the Elden Ring movie will be like in terms of plot, characters, and lore, but if these first set images are any indication of what's to come, good things are on the horizon . The journey from set construction to finished film is long and fraught with potential pitfalls, but having a passionate director, a committed studio, and involvement from both FromSoftware and George R.R. Martin gives this adaptation better odds than most.

For Filipino gamers who've poured hundreds of hours into exploring The Lands Between, defeating impossible bosses, and piecing together the game's cryptic lore, the wait until 2027-2028 will be agonizing. But if the leaked footage is any indication, that wait might just be worth it.

Elden Ring Movie Set Leak: First Look at A24's Epic Adaptation

Published on April 5, 2026 | Gaming News

The gaming world is buzzing with excitement after leaked footage of the Elden Ring movie set went viral across TikTok and Reddit. Fan-recorded footage showcases an in-the-works set with the Statue of Marika, generating over 175,000 views and 25,000 upvotes as fans get their first real glimpse of A24's ambitious live-action adaptation.

Director Alex Garland's highly anticipated film adaptation appears to be in active production somewhere in the English countryside, with sets that fans are already praising for their game-accurate detail. Here's everything we know about this massive leak and what it means for the upcoming Elden Ring movie.

Table of Contents

  1. The Leaked Footage: What We Saw

The Leaked Footage: What We Saw

The footage appears to have been recorded by a fan somewhere in the UK, and was originally posted to TikTok by user throxtv , a Twitch streamer and Elden Ring fan who stumbled upon what appears to be an active filming location.

How the Leak Happened

Throx managed to find the filming location out in the English countryside after being tipped off by an anonymous source . Throx followed up the leak with another video from the same location, lending more weight to the footage after some viewers questioned whether it was real .

Wagons, barrels, and stacks of cinderblocks can be seen, along with construction equipment . The cinderblocks visible in the footage bear the logo of Hedgehog Construction, a company known for providing "specialist scenery construction for film and television" , adding legitimacy to the leak.

Viral Social Media Response

The footage spread like wildfire across gaming communities. The leaked footage appeared on Reddit and TikTok with the caption "Elden Ring movie set spotted" and has already generated close to 175,000 views , with the Reddit post alone racking up 25,000 upvotes on the game's dedicated subreddit .

Note: As of publication, A24 has not issued any official statement confirming or denying the authenticity of the leaked footage, nor have the videos been taken down from social media platforms.

Set Details and Game Accuracy

The Church of Marika Recreation

The centerpiece of the leaked footage is undeniably the Church of Marika reconstruction. The footage presents what looks like a near-exact physical recreation of one of Elden Ring's most recognizable points of interest . The Church of Marika, scattered across The Lands Between in various states of ruin, is an immediately identifiable location for anyone who has spent serious time with FromSoftware's action RPG .

What's particularly impressive is the level of detail. The biggest giveaway is the Statue of Marika, which many commenters on Reddit and social media are already praising for looking like it was adapted straight from the video game . Fans have noted that the statue appears more intact compared to the broken versions found throughout the game's DLC areas.

Environmental Design and Location

The open field it's sitting in resembles the game's opening area, Limgrave, minus the fences surrounding it . The environment closely resembles the Gothic architecture and visual style seen in the original game, suggesting a strong emphasis on authenticity in the film's design .

The architecture and statue are immediately recognisable as a Church of Marika from within the game , though the specific church hasn't been definitively identified. The set is absolutely a church of Marika with a statue that is more or less intact compared to all the broken ones in the DLC, and specifically looks like it could be the third church of Marika .

Pro Tip: For Filipino gamers unfamiliar with Elden Ring, the Church of Marika serves as a safe haven and checkpoint location in the game, similar to bonfires in Dark Souls. These churches are iconic landmarks that players encounter throughout their journey in The Lands Between.

Elden Ring Movie Production Status

Official Announcement Timeline

Bandai Namco officially announced the Elden Ring movie in May 2025, confirming that Garland would write and direct the adaptation as part of a collaboration with A24 . Since that announcement, details have been scarce—until now.

According to recent reports, casting includes Kit Connor ("Heartstopper"), Ben Whishaw ("Skyfall"), and Cailee Spaeny ("Priscilla," "Alien: Romulus"), who are all said to have the three lead roles . These casting choices suggest a mix of established talent and rising stars for the adaptation.

Alex Garland's Passionate Pitch

What makes this project particularly special is the director's dedication. Reports in 2025 said Garland pitched the project with a 160-page script and 40 pages of imagery before flying to Japan with A24 to secure approval from FromSoftware and Bandai Namco . This hands-on approach convinced fans that the film was landing with someone who genuinely loves the source material .

The filmmaker is a noted fan of the game, so much so that he whipped up a 160-page script just to convince FromSoftware and Bandai Namco that he should be the one to handle it .

Filming Location and Production Scale

Production is now underway, with reports indicating that filming is taking place in the United Kingdom . The choice of England as a filming location makes practical sense, as the production company can save on VFX costs by shooting the film in England, because now they won't have to alter the weather in post to make things look dark and miserable .

Confirmed Cast and Creative Team

Director: Alex Garland

The project will be led by filmmaker Alex Garland (Ex Machina, Civil War, Warfare) as writer and director . Garland's track record with cerebral science fiction and intense dramatic works suggests a darker, more thoughtful approach to the Elden Ring universe.

Producers and Creative Consultants

The film will be produced by Peter Rice alongside Andrew Macdonald and Allon Reich from DNA, and George R. R. Martin and Vince Gerardis . Having George R.R. Martin involved as a producer is particularly significant, as ELDEN RING was created under the guidance of FromSoftware's Hidetaka Miyazaki, based on a mythological story written by George R. R. Martin .

Reported Cast Members

According to IMDb and industry reports, the confirmed cast includes:

  • Cailee Spaeny - Known for "Priscilla" and "Alien: Romulus," cast in an undisclosed lead role
  • Ben Whishaw - "Skyfall" star in an undisclosed lead role
  • Kit Connor - "Heartstopper" breakout star in an undisclosed lead role

What the Sets Reveal About the Story

Post-Shattering Setting

The condition of the Church of Marika in the leaked footage provides important narrative clues. The Church of Marika shown in the clip appears in a post-Shattering state, meaning broken and abandoned rather than intact, which points toward the movie being set after the Shattering event rather than serving as a prequel .

This detail is significant for fans who were hoping for a backstory. Some fans had hoped for a prequel built around George R.R. Martin's original worldbuilding, which covers events predating the game entirely, but the destroyed ruins in this clip suggest otherwise .

Potential Story Focus

Curiously, there's been no official talk so far about Elden Ring adapting the story of FromSoftware's 2023 classic, nor the side narrative found in the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC, though even if it doesn't follow the source material directly, there's plenty for the Elden Ring movie to get stuck into .

George R.R. Martin had a considerable hand in shaping The Lands Between, penning much of the world's history and backstory – essentially, everything before the player's Tarnished arrives is the product of the Game of Thrones author .

Popular Fan Theories

One character frequently mentioned in speculation is Vyke. Vyke is currently one of the most-requested characters that fans want to lead an Elden Ring movie, as like the player's Tarnished, he had worked to restore the Elden Ring and rule over The Lands Between as the Elden Lord .

Release Date and Timeline

Expected Release Window

Announced in May 2025, the Elden Ring movie is currently undated . Based on typical production timelines for films of this scale, 2027 could be realistic – depending on when filming begins .

It's likely the film won't see the light of day until 2027 at earliest, and the recently leaked movie set footage is a good indication that the ball is already rolling in the right direction in terms of production timeline .

Director's Schedule

Working in its favor is the fact that director Alex Garland only has the third 28 Years Later film in the works now the 2025 movie and its sequel The Bone Temple have been released , meaning he can dedicate significant attention to Elden Ring.

Why the Wait?

The lengthy production timeline makes sense given the scope of the project. It suggests the need for an enormous visual effects budget to do the game justice . For reference, Garland with "Civil War" directed the biggest budgeted movie in A24's history, which brought in $127.3 million worldwide .

content:
Elden Ring Movie Set Leak: First Look at A24's Epic Adaptation Surfaces Online

Published April 5, 2026 | Updated just now

Breaking News: Leaked set footage from the Elden Ring movie has gone viral across TikTok and Reddit, showing what appears to be a game-accurate Church of Marika statue. The footage has already racked up over 175,000 views and 25,000 Reddit upvotes, sparking massive excitement among FromSoftware fans worldwide.

The Elden Ring community is experiencing a collective meltdown as newly surfaced fan-recorded footage of the Elden Ring movie set has started to circulate on various social media channels such as Reddit and TikTok . The footage appears to have been recorded by a fan somewhere in the UK, and was originally posted to TikTok by user throxtv , offering what may be our first legitimate glimpse into Alex Garland and A24's ambitious live-action adaptation of FromSoftware's critically acclaimed action RPG.

For a game that has sold more than 30 million units worldwide and won over 300 Game of the Year awards, the stakes for this cinematic adaptation couldn't be higher. Now, thanks to an eagle-eyed fan in the English countryside, we're getting our first taste of what The Lands Between might look like on the big screen—and the early signs are incredibly promising.

Table of Contents

  1. What the Leaked Footage Shows
  2. Fan Reaction: "Literally Ripped from the Game"
  3. Production Details and Filming Location
  4. Everything We Know About the Elden Ring Movie
  5. When Will the Elden Ring Movie Release?
  6. What the Set Reveals About the Film's Direction
  7. Why This Leak Matters for Video Game Adaptations
  8. Frequently Asked Questions

What the Leaked Footage Shows

The footage showcases an in-the-works set with the Statue of Marika and a stone structure that fans such as omgItsGhostDog say looks like "was literally ripped straight from the game" . The viral clips reveal a detailed physical reconstruction of one of Elden Ring's most iconic landmarks: a Church of Marika.

The Church of Marika Recreation

The video allegedly shows a Church of Marika set, seemingly built somewhere in the British countryside, and it's already pulled in over 25,000 upvotes on the game's dedicated subreddit. The footage presents what looks like a near-exact physical recreation of one of Elden Ring's most recognizable points of interest .

For players familiar with the game, these churches are sacred spaces scattered throughout The Lands Between. The Church of Marika, scattered across The Lands Between in various states of ruin, is an immediately identifiable location for anyone who has spent serious time with FromSoftware's action RPG . They serve as grace sites, lore repositories, and atmospheric touchstones that define the game's melancholic tone.

What's Visible in the Videos

According to detailed fan analysis, the leaked footage shows:

  • The Statue of Marika: The biggest giveaway is the Statue of Marika, which many commenters on Reddit and social media are already praising for looking like it was adapted straight from the video game
  • Crumbling stonework: Weathered architecture matching the game's post-Shattering aesthetic
  • Ruined church structure: Stone walls and archways in various states of decay
  • Production equipment: Wagons, barrels, and stacks of cinderblocks can be seen, along with construction equipment
Fun Fact: The cinderblocks visible in the second leaked video bear the logo of Hedgehog Construction, a UK company known for providing specialist scenery construction for film and television productions.

Fan Reaction: "Literally Ripped from the Game"

The response from the Elden Ring community has been nothing short of euphoric. The leaked footage has fans absolutely hyped for what's to come from Alex Garland and A24 when further official details about the Elden Ring movie start to trickle out .

Social Media Explodes with Excitement

The leaked footage appeared on Reddit and TikTok with the caption "Elden Ring movie set spotted" and has already generated close to 175,000 views . The Reddit thread on r/Eldenring became one of the most upvoted posts in recent weeks, with thousands of comments dissecting every frame of the footage.

Common reactions include:

  • "This actually looks game-accurate—I'm starting to believe this could work"
  • "The attention to detail on that Marika statue is insane"
  • "If they're putting this much effort into the sets, imagine the actual cinematography"
  • "Alex Garland understands the assignment"

Why Fans Are So Relieved

Video game movie adaptations have a notoriously poor track record. From the catastrophic Super Mario Bros. (1993) to more recent misfires, fans have learned to temper expectations. However, this leak suggests A24 and Garland are taking a different approach—one focused on authenticity and respect for the source material.

The fact that reports in 2025 said Garland pitched the project with a 160-page script and 40 pages of imagery before flying to Japan with A24 to secure approval from FromSoftware and Bandai Namco already demonstrated serious commitment. These set leaks provide visual confirmation that the production is following through on that promise.

Production Details and Filming Location

Where Is the Elden Ring Movie Being Filmed?

Production is now underway, with reports indicating that filming is taking place in the United Kingdom . This makes perfect sense given the UK's abundance of Gothic architecture, medieval ruins, and landscapes that naturally evoke The Lands Between's atmosphere.

The leak appears to have started with TikTok user THROX, also known as @throxtv, who posted video from a fenced-off site in the English countryside. Throx followed up the leak with another video from the same location, lending more weight to the footage after some viewers questioned whether it was real .

Construction Company Involvement

The presence of Hedgehog Construction equipment on-site is particularly telling. This company specializes in creating elaborate scenery for major film and television productions. Their portfolio includes work on blockbuster franchises and high-budget fantasy series, suggesting A24 is sparing no expense on practical effects and physical sets.

How Much of the Film Will Use Practical Sets?

While it's too early to know the exact balance between practical effects and CGI, the investment in physical construction suggests Garland is prioritizing tangible, real-world environments wherever possible. This approach aligns with his previous work on films like Ex Machina and Annihilation, which skillfully blended practical and digital effects.

Verification Status: While the footage is highly compelling and matches known production details, A24 has not officially confirmed or denied the authenticity of these leaks. The videos have not been taken down, which many interpret as a positive sign.

Everything We Know About the Elden Ring Movie

Bandai Namco Entertainment Inc. and A24 announced their collaboration with writer and director Alex Garland for a live-action film adaptation of FromSoftware Inc.'s world-renowned video game ELDEN RING in May 2025, nearly a year ago.

Director: Alex Garland

To bring the epic world and intense action of ELDEN RING to film, the project will be led by filmmaker Alex Garland (Ex Machina, Civil War, Warfare) as writer and director . Garland's track record is exceptional:

Film Year Notable Achievement
Ex Machina 2015 Oscar win for Best Visual Effects
Annihilation 2018 Acclaimed sci-fi horror with 88% RT score
Civil War 2024 Box office success, critical darling
Warfare 2025 Recent A24 collaboration

Garland is known for cerebral, atmospheric filmmaking that prioritizes mood and tension over constant action—an approach that could translate perfectly to Elden Ring's methodical, contemplative storytelling style.

Production Team

The film will be produced by Peter Rice alongside Andrew Macdonald and Allon Reich from DNA, and George R. R. Martin and Vince Gerardis . The involvement of George R.R. Martin is particularly significant—he created much of the worldbuilding and backstory for the game itself, writing the mythological history that preceded the player's journey.

Potential Cast Members

While no official casting announcements have been made, independent entertainment journalist Jeff Sneider reported last year that actors Ben Whishaw (Skyfall), Kit Connor (Heartstopper), and Cailee Spaeny (Civil War) were "in talks" to star in the film .

When Will the Elden Ring Movie Release?

It's likely the film won't see the light of day until 2027 at earliest, and the recently leaked movie set footage is a good indication that the ball is already rolling in the right direction in terms of production timeline .

Production Timeline Breakdown

Based on typical production schedules for films of this scope:

  • Now (April 2026): Principal photography appears to be underway or beginning soon
  • Mid-Late 2026: Filming completion expected
  • 2026-2027: Post-production, visual effects, sound design
  • Late 2027 or 2028: Theatrical release

Announced in May 2025, the Elden Ring movie is currently undated. Frankly, that means Elden Ring is a fair way off from coming to cinemas. 2027 could be realistic – depending on when filming begins .

What Could Delay or Accelerate the Timeline?

Several factors could impact the release date:

  • Visual effects complexity: Elden Ring's fantastical creatures and magic systems will require extensive CGI work
  • A24's release strategy: The studio may target specific awards season windows or summer blockbuster slots
  • Director's schedule: Alex Garland only has the third 28 Years Later film in the works now the 2025 movie and its sequel The Bone Temple have been released

What the Set Reveals About the Film's Direction

Post-Shattering Setting Confirmed?

One of the most intriguing analytical details from the leaked footage concerns the timeline. Assuming the footage is real, the choice of location carries some narrative weight. The Church of Marika shown in the clip appears in a post-Shattering state, meaning broken and abandoned rather than intact. That points toward the movie being set after the Shattering event rather than serving as a prequel depicting The Lands Between before Ranni stole a fragment of the Rune of Death .

This suggests the film might follow a narrative similar to the game—a Tarnished returning to The Lands Between after the world has fallen into ruin—rather than depicting the golden age before the Elden Ring was shattered.

Emphasis on Practical Locations

Images and clips circulating on social media appear to show large-scale set construction, including a detailed replica of Queen Marika's statue. The environment closely resembles the Gothic architecture and visual style seen in the original game, suggesting a strong emphasis on authenticity in the film's design .

This practical approach could yield several benefits:

  • Tangible atmosphere: Real sets provide actors with immersive environments and give cinematographers physical textures to work with
  • Visual coherence: Blending practical sets with digital extensions often produces more convincing results than pure CGI environments
  • Cost efficiency: While expensive upfront, practical sets can reduce VFX workload in post-production

Garland's Vision for the Story

There's been no (official) talk so far about Elden Ring adapting the story of FromSoftware's 2023 classic, nor the side narrative found in the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC. Even if it doesn't follow the source material directly, there's plenty for the Elden Ring movie to get stuck into – largely thanks to a familiar person behind its lore .

George R.R. Martin's involvement as both original worldbuilder and producer suggests the film might explore aspects of the lore that were only hinted at in the game. George R.R. Martin had a considerable hand in shaping The Lands Between, penning much of the world's history and backstory – essentially, everything before the player's Tarnished arrives is the product of the Game of Thrones author .

The Video Game Movie Renaissance

We're living through a golden age for video game adaptations. Recent successes include:

  • Fallout (Amazon) - Massive viewership and renewal
  • The Super Mario Bros. Movie - $1.36 billion box office
  • Sonic the Hedgehog franchise - Three successful films and counting

The Elden Ring movie arrives at a moment when studios finally understand that respecting source material and hiring passionate creators yields better results than cynical cash-grabs.

A24's Track Record with Genre Films

A24 has established itself as the premier studio for elevated genre filmmaking. Their approach combines artistic ambition with commercial viability, producing films like Hereditary, Midsommar, Everything Everywhere All at Once, and Civil War.

This pedigree suggests the Elden Ring movie won't be a generic fantasy action film, but something more thoughtful and atmospheric—much like the game itself.

What Success Could Mean for FromSoftware Adaptations

If the Elden Ring movie succeeds, it could open doors for other FromSoftware properties:

  • Bloodborne: Victorian Gothic horror ripe for adaptation
  • Sekiro: Samurai action with a clear narrative structure
  • Dark Souls: The original Soulsborne experience
  • Armored Core: Mecha action for a different audience

The game has achieved remarkable commercial success, with this title released on February 25, 2022, and has shipped more than 30 million units worldwide . That massive player base represents a built-in audience eager to see The Lands Between on the big screen.

The Philippine Gaming Connection

Elden Ring has a passionate following in the Philippines, where FromSoftware's challenging games have cultivated a dedicated community. Filipino gamers have organized speedrun competitions, lore discussion groups, and PvP tournaments around the title.

The movie's eventual release will likely see strong Philippine box office performance, continuing the country's enthusiasm for fantasy epics and video game adaptations. Local gaming cafes and communities are already discussing the leaked footage, with many planning viewing parties for the film's premiere.

The Road Ahead for Elden Ring on Screen

Ultimately, fans have no clue what the Elden Ring movie will be like in terms of plot, characters, and lore, but if these first set images are any indication of what's to come, good things are on the horizon .

The leaked footage represents far more than a simple construction site video—it's a proof of concept that demonstrates A24 and Alex Garland's commitment to bringing The Lands Between to life with authenticity and care. The overwhelmingly positive fan reaction suggests the production is on the right track.

As we await official announcements, trailers, and eventually the film itself, these leaked images serve as a tantalizing preview of what could be one of the most significant video game adaptations ever attempted. With a director who genuinely loves the source material, a studio known for artistic excellence, and George R.R. Martin's continued involvement, all the pieces are in place for something special.

Elden Ring Movie Set Leak Reveals First Look at Alex Garland's A24 Epic

Published April 5, 2026 | Last Updated: April 5, 2026

Breaking News: Leaked footage from the Elden Ring movie set has gone viral, showing game-accurate recreations of iconic locations. The videos have generated over 175,000 views across TikTok and Reddit since April 3, 2026.

The internet is ablaze with excitement after leaked footage appeared on Reddit and TikTok with the caption "Elden Ring movie set spotted" and has already generated close to 175,000 views . For fans of FromSoftware's masterpiece, this represents the first tangible proof that Alex Garland's highly anticipated A24 adaptation is moving forward—and it looks absolutely stunning.

The Elden Ring film adaptation was first announced back in May 2025 by A24 and Bandai Namco , but since then, details have been scarce. Now, thanks to a lucky discovery in the English countryside, we finally have our first glimpse of The Lands Between brought to life.

Table of Contents

  1. The Leaked Set Footage: What We Saw
  2. How the Set Was Discovered
  3. Elden Ring Movie: Everything We Know
  4. Current Production Status and Timeline
  5. Fan Reactions and Community Response
  6. What This Means for Video Game Adaptations
